briefs.episode_title: THE GREAT SAVE! ⚜ Stock Market Defies the Breakdown
briefs.one_line_summary: Technical trend lines saved the S\&P 500 from a breakdown, reinforcing a "buy the dip" mentality, though a painful correction remains a risk.
core_claims: S\&P 500 bounced back from plunging below a key trendline, closing above it, suggesting a potential psychological entrenchment of dip-buying.
ISM PMI Services data release tomorrow may cause market movements; technical analysis points to possible support and resistance levels.
IWM (Russell 2000) broke its current trend, needing a move lower than $253 to confirm further selling pressure; a potential head and shoulders pattern targets $241.78.
Gold declined to its support level at $5,078, with the potential to tag $4,845 if current levels break.
Silver's failed breakout above a parallel channel implies further downside, targeting an inclining trendline at $61.40.
US Oil closed above a trendline from July 8th, requiring it to get above $77.98 for confirmation; geopolitical tension could impact oil prices.
Natural Gas hit resistance at its inclining parallel channel, signaling caution for further breakthroughs and potential consolidation.

MongoDB had a double beat but the stock got slammed, facing serious selling pressure; could bounce on the daily chart, near-term resistance $256.89 with downside support at $205.82 and ultimate destination $151.
Sea Limited (SE) had a double beat before market open, but price action plunged; first resistance decline trend line to watch at $91.84, with potential gap fill and retest to $115.61.
Pinterest popped on Elliott Investment Management investing a billion dollars and that Elliott will get prices for pinterest at $22.72 a share; testing trendlines at $25.60 and $26.50.
Microsoft secured itself back inside its parallel channel, Microsoft may come up and tag a resistance trendline right around $440.
briefs.positioning_implication: Be vigilant for trend reversals and confirmation signals. Monitor key support/resistance levels for potential entries/exits.

briefs.episode_title: Iran War Rocks Markets; Investor Says 3 Sectors Are About To Explode | Tavi Costa
briefs.one_line_summary: Gold and silver price appreciation is not being linked to hyperinflation risks, while an Iranian war potentially impacts broader markets.
core_claims: Energy prices are unlikely to be cheap going forward, causing inflationary pressures.
The US economy requires lower interest rates due to interest payment relative to GDP nearing 4-5%.
The market is complacent to geopolitical risks; oil is moving significantly, and energy is unlikely to be cheap.
Investment regime shift: emphasis towards bottom-line valuations and value investing returning due to higher risk in emerging markets.
Mining stocks are currently 1% of global equities, compared to 11% in the 1970s, indicating undervaluation.
Investors worldwide have less confidence in US dollar assets because a large amount of capital has already been parked in the US.
Silver prices will reach triple digits.
briefs.positioning_implication: Shift investment focus to undervalued resources and emerging markets, recognizing a potential sea change in global financial leadership.

briefs.episode_title: Earning's Central Live 🎬 - ROST, BOX, CRWD
briefs.one_line_summary: Live trading of earnings reports for ROST, BOX, and CRWD focuses on pre-identified key levels and managing risk amidst market volatility.
core_claims: Earnings per share target for ROST is $1.87 to $1.90, a 4.5-6% year-over-year increase.
Revenue expectation for ROST is an increase of 7.8-9%, equating to about $6.4 billion.
ROST support levels are $188 (aggressive), $183 (secondary), and $175 (conservative).
ROST resistance levels are $206.29 (double top), $211.70, and $220.
BOX earnings are expected down 21-22%, with negative earnings.
BOX support levels are $21.82, $21.19, and $20.72.
BOX resistance levels are $25.90, $26.47, and $27.13.
CrashStrike support is at $354.40, $342.36, and $334
CrashStrike resistance is at $415.30, $433, and $439
briefs.positioning_implication: Implement level-based trading strategies, adjusting positions based on market reactions and risk tolerance to capitalize on earnings-driven volatility.

briefs.episode_title: Gold & Silver Crash Again As Iran War Gets Ugly Quickly
briefs.one_line_summary: Geopolitical tensions catalyze a broad market sell-off affecting precious metals.
core_claims: Gold futures are down $202 today, $14 since Sunday night's open.
Silver is down $6 today.
True market sell-offs affect all assets except treasuries.
Retail interest in gold and silver indicates a later-stage market development.
Silver could outperform gold in the next 2-3 years.
Gold was up 57% year-over-year, contributing to margin expansion.
briefs.positioning_implication: Reduce exposure to broad market assets; re-evaluate allocations for precious metals miners based on individual company performance.

briefs.episode_title: THIS is BIG! 🦍🦍 SILVER Just had a MASSIVE Change... (PRICE SMASH!) Gold - Precious Metals News
briefs.one_line_summary: A publicly traded company is adding physical silver to its treasury management, while the Shanghai Gold Exchange reports zero silver inventory.
core_claims: $1 trillion was wiped out from the precious metals markets in 80 minutes.
Gold fell 2% in 80 minutes, erasing $740 billion.
Silver dropped 5.4% in 80 minutes, erasing $2.6 billion.
Since January 26th, 2025, silver has had five days with $10-$15 intraday ranges, three days with $15-$20 ranges, one day with a $20-$25 range, and one day with a $44 range.
A publicly traded company is adding silver to its treasury management in a significant amount.
The Shanghai Gold Exchange weekly report shows zero silver inventory figures across all lines.
briefs.positioning_implication: Consider diversifying treasury assets into physical silver; monitor Shanghai Gold Exchange data for supply stress signals.

briefs.episode_title: Gold Surges on Middle East Escalation. Pullback Risk Ahead? | James Thorne
briefs.one_line_summary: Temporary gold price spike driven by Middle East tensions and potential pullback due to market overreaction and potential leadership changes.
core_claims: Long-term investors should anticipate a "peace dividend" and market calm within 12-14 months.
Market may be overly crowded with investors seeing gold/silver as a 'safety trade' rather than a 'debasement trade,' leading to a potential pullback when Middle East tensions ease.
Gold is projected to reach $10,000 by the end of the decade, after digesting the current price run.
Technology, particularly Bitcoin and Ethereum, may attract capital away from defensive trades like gold and energy.
Interest rate cuts primarily benefit interest-rate-sensitive sectors like housing.
A new global monetary system is going to be blockchain-based and incorporate both Bitcoin and gold, acting as a neutral base layer.
briefs.positioning_implication: Reduce exposure to crowded 'safety' trades and reallocate towards oversold growth sectors, particularly in anticipation of geopolitical de-escalation.  Monitor for shifts in market leadership and policy adjustments.

briefs.episode_title: Regime Change, Forever Wars, and the MADNESS of the 'Elite': Marc Faber
briefs.one_line_summary: The U.S. and Israel's attack on Iran will likely prolong conflict in the Middle East, potentially involving Russia and China, and causing volatility in financial markets.
core_claims: Wars initiated by ignorant people last particularly long.
The Iranian population mistrusts the US and would not want Israeli or US occupation.
Emerging markets are at the beginning of a bull cycle.
The war will provide an excuse for the Federal Reserve to print money, weakening the dollar.
Bonds are not attractive because they are low yielding instruments.
briefs.positioning_implication: Exercise caution in financial assets and prioritize diversification across geographical regions and asset classes, including precious metals.

briefs.episode_title: What Trump’s War With Iran Means for China and Global Oil | China Decode
briefs.channel: The Prof G Pod – Scott Galloway
briefs.one_line_summary: Geopolitical shifts impacting China's strategic positioning and oil security are considered in light of US actions in the Middle East and escalating US-China tensions.
core_claims: The Shanghai Asia Index rose about 0.5% while the Hang Seng Asia index fell almost 3%, the largest sell-off since the liberation day tariff announcement last April.
As the Middle East teeters after the killing of Ali Khameni, China is no longer just quietly watching but openly condemning Washington and positioning itself for what comes next.
China has been pretty slow and restrained in its language, and hasn't condemned the strikes as strongly as expected.
15% of seaborne crude going to China comes from Iran, and Chinese are definitely worried about the Strait of Hormuz.
China purchases about 80% of Iran's oil, which is about 13% of China's total seaborne oil imports.
China intends to increase AI adoption in its economy to over 90% by 2030, to be completely transformed by AI by 2035.
briefs.positioning_implication: Assess exposure to geopolitical risks, particularly those affecting energy supply chains and the need for strategic partnerships with US rival states.

briefs.episode_title: Dario Amodei Made One Mistake. Sam Altman Got $110 Billion. Here's the Full Story.
briefs.channel: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
briefs.one_line_summary: Anthropic's principled stance against unrestricted military AI use contrasted sharply with OpenAI's defense deals, impacting industry power dynamics.
core_claims: Anthropic's enterprise revenue was growing faster than OpenAI's, but the Pentagon deal and funding round significantly shifted the balance.
US Central Command used Anthropic's Claude for intelligence assessment and combat simulations against Iran.
OpenAI's deal includes red lines: no mass domestic surveillance, autonomous weapons, or social credit systems.
OpenAI secured a $110 billion funding round valuing the company at $730B pre-money and $840B post-money.
OpenAI annual revenue is projected to reach $100B by 2029 and $280B by 2030, despite projected losses of $14B this year.
Stargate, a joint venture with SoftBank, Oracle, and Abu Dhabi's MGX, aims for $500B in AI infrastructure and 10 GW capacity by 2029.
briefs.positioning_implication: Evaluate AI vendor relationships based on long-term strategic alignment and potential regulatory impacts, beyond short-term performance metrics. Recognize government contracts are becoming the most durable revenue in technology.

briefs.episode_title: Market Fear Spikes, Institutional Analysis Revealed: Oil Surges, Yields Pop, Market Trap Door Opens
briefs.one_line_summary: Geopolitical tensions and resulting market volatility create short-term trading opportunities based on technical analysis.
core_claims: US saying that military action could be over a month long.
Straits of Hormuz are effectively closed due to unaffordable insurance costs, despite US denials.
10-year yield spiking indicates rising inflation expectations amidst geopolitical tensions.
Parallel lines on S&P and Nasdaq charts signal potential topping and distribution phases, warning of limited upside.
S&P 6790 is a critical level; closing below it could trigger a waterfall event.
Silver's failed breakout confirms a bearish flag pattern; gold is under pressure after hitting resistance at 5400.
briefs.positioning_implication: Reduce long exposure in equities and precious metals. Consider short-term long positions in the US dollar and potential short positions in oil above $80, carefully managed with defined stop-loss levels.

briefs.episode_title: S&P 500 Support & Resistance Levels Breaking Down — What Traders Need to Watch Right Now
briefs.one_line_summary: Key S&P 500 and QQQ support levels are breaking down, necessitating a revised trading strategy based on new support and resistance points.
core_claims: ORCL is being eyed for long positions at $136.48, representing a solid technical support level.
LITE experienced a 6.5% drop from highs, offering potential opportunities contingent on Fibonacci retracement levels.
Aggressive traders can short SPY at $676.57, while a long position is considered at $671.40.
Dollar-cost averaging into QQQ is recommended, targeting an average down to $583.46.
Nvidia's long position is eyed at $171.88, with dollar-cost averaging down to $166.90, adjusting every $1.70 or approximately 1% drop.
ONON may experience a further drop to $35.17 if pre-market lows of $39.99 are breached, with potential for a significant 11-12% decline.
briefs.positioning_implication: Shift to a tactical approach, factoring in revised support and resistance to capitalize on potential moves. Implement dollar-cost averaging strategies to mitigate risk in volatile assets.

briefs.episode_title: Frank Giustra & Ian Harris: $6.00 Copper, $1.5B Buyout & The "Gun-Shy" Majors Paying The Price
briefs.one_line_summary: Majors' hesitancy to acquire early-stage copper assets will result in paying significantly higher prices, exacerbated by increased demand from AI and strategic stock-piling.
core_claims: Majors overpay for acquisitions due to risk aversion, driven by job security concerns, making them less agile than in the past.
Copper price of $6 remains stable, increasing the junior tier one entry point starts at $15.
A potential supply deficit of 2 million tons of copper by 2030 and 8 million tons by 2035 is projected, exacerbated by disruptions in supply chains like the DRC.
AI's copper demand is estimated at 450,000 tons, the equivalent of the world's second-largest copper mine.
briefs.positioning_implication: Shift focus towards early-stage, large-scale copper assets, anticipating strategic acquisitions driven by supply deficits and rising demand, particularly in jurisdictions perceived as undervalued.

briefs.episode_title: Meaningful Tailwinds" To Push Stocks Higher Over Coming Months? | Darius Dale
briefs.one_line_summary: The growth, monetary policy, and liquidity cycles indicate tailwinds for risk assets, leading to investor satisfaction and outperformance in international, small-cap, and cyclical equities.
core_claims: Investors with equities and credit will likely be satisfied by year-end, especially with international, small-cap, and cyclical equities.
The current risk-on regime rewards increasing portfolio risk.
US equities appear to be in a topping process, but international equities and US equity factors have not.
The US dollar is in a secular bear market due to geopolitical supply/demand imbalances in the treasury debt market.
AI diffusion will converge productivity, profitability, and earnings growth across sectors and geographies.
The US is transitioning from a 1.5-2% trend productivity economy to a 3% trend productivity economy.
briefs.positioning_implication: Shift portfolio allocations towards international equities, small-cap stocks, and cyclical factors to capitalize on expected outperformance. Consider systematic approaches to narrow information asymmetry and elevate investment behavior.

briefs.episode_title: War With Iran: Why Oil Didn’t Spike As Expected | Prof G Markets
briefs.channel: The Prof G Pod – Scott Galloway
briefs.one_line_summary: Markets showed surprising resilience despite US/Israel strikes on Iran, though prices are expected to be highly volatile.
core_claims: Brent crude oil jumped 7% to $77/barrel after US/Israel strikes on Iran, but did not spike as expected; already up 17% this year.
Diesel prices increased 15% following attacks on Saudi Arabia's Ras Tanura refinery.
Natural gas prices in Europe have risen up to 50% due to an attack on a Qatari LNG terminal.
A third of global seaborne fertilizer exports originate from the Middle East, indicating substantial supply chain risk.
briefs.positioning_implication: Expect increased market volatility due to geopolitical uncertainty and prepare for potential supply chain disruptions. Evaluate investments with an emphasis on safety, prioritizing physical, technological, personal, and financial security.

core_claims: Anthropic's enterprise revenue was growing faster than OpenAI's, but the Pentagon deal and funding round significantly shifted the balance.
US Central Command used Anthropic's Claude for intelligence assessment and combat simulations against Iran.
OpenAI's deal includes red lines: no mass domestic surveillance, autonomous weapons, or social credit systems.
OpenAI secured a $110 billion funding round valuing the company at $730B pre-money and $840B post-money.
OpenAI annual revenue is projected to reach $100B by 2029 and $280B by 2030, despite projected losses of $14B this year.
Stargate, a joint venture with SoftBank, Oracle, and Abu Dhabi's MGX, aims for $500B in AI infrastructure and 10 GW capacity by 2029.
briefs.positioning_implication: Evaluate AI vendor relationships based on long-term strategic alignment and potential regulatory impacts, beyond short-term performance metrics. Recognize government contracts are becoming the most durable revenue in technology.
